mission: Define fair and partial library baseline guidance for fused INT4 dequant GEMV so agents compare custom kernels against materialized dequant, bitsandbytes, Composable Kernel/CK Tile, hipBLASLt, MIGraphX, vLLM on ROCm, Transformer Engine on ROCm, Triton, and framework-generated paths without changing the quantization contract silently.

weights.packing_order: even k in low nibble, odd k in high nibble
weights.odd_cols_policy: unused high nibble in final byte is ignored

groups.tail_policy: final group may contain fewer than group_size K values

scales.layout: row-major by output row then contiguous K group

zero_points.layout: one metadata byte per output row and K group

seed_contract.accumulator_dtype: fp32 in CUDA seed; fp64 in CPU oracle before final fp32 cast

tolerance.reference: CPU oracle with fp64 accumulation and final fp32 output

fairness_gate.if_not_matched: Use a partial, not-applicable, library-win-different-contract, negative-example, or correctness-fail label instead of a fair win label.

result_labels.label: custom-win-fair
result_labels.meaning: Custom kernel beats the closest direct library path under the same quantization, math, output, epilogue, tolerance, and timing contract.

result_labels.label: custom-specialized-win-fair
result_labels.meaning: Custom kernel beats a fair library path for a narrower fixed shape, layout, epilogue, architecture, or runtime boundary.

result_labels.label: near-match-fair
result_labels.meaning: Custom and library results are within measurement noise under the same contract.

result_labels.label: library-win-fair
result_labels.meaning: Library path wins under the same contract; record why and what narrower custom specialization remains plausible.

result_labels.label: partial-win-materialized-dequant-only
result_labels.meaning: Custom fused kernel beats dequantize-to-FP16-or-FP32 plus GEMV/GEMM, but a direct low-bit library competitor has not been measured.

result_labels.label: partial-win-format-conversion
result_labels.meaning: Custom kernel beats a baseline that required changing quantization format, packing, scale layout, zero-point layout, or output dtype.

result_labels.label: partial-win-framework-overhead-only
result_labels.meaning: Custom path wins only when framework dispatch, allocation, conversion, or extra launches are included in the compared baseline.

result_labels.label: library-win-different-contract
result_labels.meaning: Library is faster, but its format or numerical contract differs from the seed contract.

result_labels.label: not-applicable
result_labels.meaning: Library path cannot express the seed contract without material conversion or custom code.

result_labels.label: negative-example
result_labels.meaning: Attempted baseline or custom path is slower, unsupported, or unhelpful, and the reason is recorded.

result_labels.label: correctness-fail
result_labels.meaning: Timing result is invalid because output did not satisfy the declared tolerance.

baseline_families.role: educational baseline and correctness-adjacent performance floor
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: direct but not strongest low-bit library baseline
fair_contract_requirements: dequant kernel uses seed byte addressing and nibble order
zero point is one uint8 storage element per row/group and masked with 0x0f
scale is fp32 per row/group
temporary dtype and layout are recorded
downstream GEMV/GEMM layout and transpose are recorded
total timing includes dequantization plus GEMV/GEMM when claiming fused advantage
notes: A win here does not prove a library win because the fused kernel avoids a materialized temporary.
Use seed FP32 tolerance for FP32 temporary; define a separate tolerance for FP16 or BF16 temporary.

baseline_families.role: low-bit framework library competitor and format reference
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: usually partial because common bitsandbytes 4-bit paths use fp4 or nf4 codebooks plus absmax metadata rather than affine uint4 zero-points
fair_contract_requirements: quant_type and codebook match the declared task contract
blocksize and metadata granularity match group_size or the difference is labeled
activation dtype and output dtype are recorded
weight orientation and transposed fast path are recorded
fallback path for non-multiple blocksize is recorded
bias and framework dispatch timing are recorded
notes: For the seed affine uint4 task, do not treat nf4 or fp4 as exact int4 without documenting the format change.
The bitsandbytes gemv_4bit CUDA path returns the activation dtype, so FP32 seed output requires FP32 activation or a separate output contract.

baseline_families.role: competitor and custom-kernel extension surface for low-bit GEMV/GEMM
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: direct only if the selected or modified path implements affine uint4 row/group scales and zero-points; otherwise reference or partial for int4, fp8, fp4, or block-scaled formats
fair_contract_requirements: operand element types and signedness match
packing order matches seed byte/nibble order or a conversion is labeled
scale factor dtype and layout match seed row/group layout
zero-point support and layout match seed contract
accumulator and output dtype match
epilogue semantics match
tile, scheduler, stages, alignment, and architecture target are recorded
notes: Stock block-scaled FP4 or FP8 examples are not exact affine uint4 GEMV seed baselines.
A modified Composable Kernel kernel still counts as custom-kernel work when the agent changes layout, iterator, epilogue, or scheduling.

baseline_families.role: vendor GEMM baseline where dtype and scale contracts are expressible
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: usually materialized-dequant or different-contract for packed affine uint4 GEMV
fair_contract_requirements: hipBLASLt supports the declared dtype and scale layout directly, or the comparison is labeled materialized/converted
matrix layouts, leading dimensions, and transpose flags are recorded
compute type, scale type, output type, epilogue, and TF32 policy match
workspace cap, heuristic count, and selected algorithm are recorded
notes: Do not claim a fair hipBLASLt INT4 comparison if packed uint4 weights and row/group zero-points are first converted to another format.
hipBLASLt is still the right baseline for materialized dequant and for future INT8, FP8, or block-scaled matmul tasks where supported.

baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: fair only through an engine or plugin that preserves the seed contract; otherwise deployment or converted-contract baseline
fair_contract_requirements: engine or plugin consumes the same packed weights, scales, zero-points, and activation dtype
output dtype and tolerance match or are separately declared
plugin enqueue, engine layer timing, engine runtime, and end-to-end serving boundaries are separated
engine build time is not mixed with runtime unless explicitly studying build overhead
notes: A MIGraphX plugin wrapping the custom kernel is an integration baseline, not proof that a standalone library path exists.
Separate isolated kernel timing from engine or serving timing.

baseline_families.role: LLM inference engine, plugin, kernel, and serving baseline
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: fair when W4A16/AWQ/weight-only/plugin path matches seed affine uint4 group metadata; otherwise deployment or format-family baseline
fair_contract_requirements: quantization algorithm, group size, zero-point policy, and packing order match
scale layout and activation scale layout are recorded
engine/plugin output dtype and tolerance match
batching, sequence lengths, KV-cache policy, and scheduler are recorded for serving metrics
engine build, checkpoint conversion, and runtime measurement are separated
notes: vLLM on ROCm quantization formats such as int4_awq, int4_wo, w4a8_awq, fp8, and nvfp4 must not be treated as equivalent to the seed without explicit format metadata.
End-to-end tokens/sec or latency can be a valid deployment result but is not the same as an isolated GEMV kernel result.

baseline_families.role: FP8, MXFP8, NVFP4, amax, and block-scaled transformer reference
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: not direct for affine uint4 GEMV; reference or different-contract baseline for FP8 and block-scaled tasks
fair_contract_requirements: task is explicitly FP8, MXFP8, NVFP4, or block-scaled
recipe, scale layout, amax history, and scale update policy are recorded
activation and weight quantizers are recorded
accumulator, bias, and output dtype are recorded
framework autocast and quantizer update timing boundary is recorded
notes: For fused-int4-dequant-gemv, Transformer Engine on ROCm should usually be labeled not-applicable unless the task is reframed as FP8 or block-scaled.
Scale and amax behavior are part of the numerical contract, not incidental metadata.

baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: direct if the Triton kernel implements the exact seed contract
fair_contract_requirements: same packed byte/nibble unpack
same row/group scale and zero-point indexing
same accumulator and output dtype
same tolerance
meta-parameters and dispatch boundary are recorded
generated LLVM IR / AMD GCN ISA claims are backed by inspected artifacts
notes: Triton can be a fair custom-kernel baseline for the seed if it preserves the exact affine uint4 contract.
Block-scaled Triton tutorials are different-contract references unless the task is reframed.

baseline_families.role: PyTorch, torch.compile, Inductor, extension, or framework eager baseline
baseline_families.applicability_to_seed: direct if the generated or eager graph preserves the exact seed expression; partial if it materializes or changes dtype/format
fair_contract_requirements: graph expression matches seed contract
materialized intermediates are recorded
compiled versus eager path is recorded
allocation, conversion, and dispatch timing boundary is recorded
correctness tolerance matches output dtype contract
notes: Framework overhead is a valid deployment concern, but it should be labeled separately from isolated kernel speed.
Do not claim compiler code-generation facts unless generated code was inspected.

must_not_claim: rocprofiler/rocprof counters unless profiler artifacts are attached
fair direct low-bit library comparison when packing, scale layout, zero-point layout, accumulator dtype, output dtype, epilogue, tolerance, or timing boundary differ
bitsandbytes nf4/fp4 equivalence to affine uint4 without documenting the format conversion
Composable Kernel block-scaled fp4/fp8 equivalence to affine uint4 without documenting the format conversion
MIGraphX or vLLM on ROCm serving win as an isolated kernel win
hipBLASLt direct int4 seed support when the path uses materialized dequant or a different dtype
